source: read-only static trace of babyworld_lite/sim.py plus aggregation of generated manifest.csv
static_trace: MATERIALS defines hardness, mass, friction, bounciness, tactile_noise per material.
sample_object copies material-derived mass/friction/bounciness/hardness into ObjectSpec with jitter.
infer_event uses mass, friction, and bounciness, but not material string or hardness.
contact_happens is sampled from action-specific miss chance; is_contact depends on contact_happens, time, distance, and radius, not material directly.
contact_force = intended_impulse * obj.hardness * noise, so force magnitude directly carries material-derived hardness.
object velocity/friction/bounce dynamics use mass, friction, and bounciness, so later contact counts/normals can be indirectly material-dependent after contact begins.
conclusion: Touch is not fully material-invariant: contact occurrence is largely material-invariant in the event/contact gate, but contact-force magnitudes directly propagate material through hardness; event logic uses material-derived mass/friction/bounciness rather than the material label itself.
